source_display: Learning Organization: Encoded Failures as Asset

Ray explained: 'Roughly 80% of people in Slack are not active — that's why email is important. If you can see who's active, it gives you a proxy for who's most engaged... If you're trying to find a sponsor, figure out who has a decision-maker title at a company headquartered in your city and who's also active.'

Ray explained: 'We're not paying you — but we're working with lawyers to figure out a way that if you raise sponsorship money (e.g., $10,000/year), the first portion goes toward your activities as needed, some portion becomes a stipend for you, and some portion goes back to FOHE global.'
FOHE uses 80% Slack inactivity not as failure metric but as targeting mechanism for identifying high-value sponsors and leaders
